subject: Add participant for policy-framework
commit_message:
Add participant for policy-framework

This commit adds a Participant for policy-framework, calls APIs
createPolicies and createPolicyTypes for creating policies and
policy-types in policy-framework when a controlLoopUpdate message is
recieved.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3038
Signed-off-by: Sirisha_Manchikanti <sirisha.manchikanti@est.tech>
Change-Id: I5ec795bbdd8684e4f6e8c65da1c340b35e7edf68

Patch Set 3:

I thought we will have a common "participant-client" library (similar to sdc distribution client) which will wrap all the communications to other tosca-controlloop components over dmaap, rest api etc.

That way integrating a new participant will be pretty generic and quick. And participants are only responsible for consuming/publishing contents using the api's exposed by the client. Thoughts?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> I thought we will have a common "participant-client" library (similar to sdc distribution client) which will wrap all the communications to other tosca-controlloop components over dmaap, rest api etc.
> 
> That way integrating a new participant will be pretty generic and quick. And participants are only responsible for consuming/publishing contents using the api's exposed by the client. Thoughts?

That makes sense to me.  Should be similar to the Actor model, with a standard set of APIs, one for ingress and another for egress.  Would also have common superclasses, like the HTTP & Topic classes in the Actor model, with common configuration properties/structures, depending on the type of superclass.  Should also support pluggable components via a META-INF file.

We should probably consider a re-design as we move forward in Istanbul.

> > 
> > That way integrating a new participant will be pretty generic and quick. And participants are only responsible for consuming/publishing contents using the api's exposed by the client. Thoughts?
> 
> That makes sense to me.  Should be similar to the Actor model, with a standard set of APIs, one for ingress and another for egress.  Would also have common superclasses, like the HTTP & Topic classes in the Actor model, with common configuration properties/structures, depending on the type of superclass.  Should also support pluggable components via a META-INF file.
> 
> We should probably consider a re-design as we move forward in Istanbul.

It makes sense to me too to make it easier on developers of participants.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 3:
> > > 
> > > I thought we will have a common "participant-client" library (similar to sdc distribution client) which will wrap all the communications to other tosca-controlloop components over dmaap, rest api etc.
> > > 
> > > That way integrating a new participant will be pretty generic and quick. And participants are only responsible for consuming/publishing contents using the api's exposed by the client. Thoughts?
> > 
> > That makes sense to me.  Should be similar to the Actor model, with a standard set of APIs, one for ingress and another for egress.  Would also have common superclasses, like the HTTP & Topic classes in the Actor model, with common configuration properties/structures, depending on the type of superclass.  Should also support pluggable components via a META-INF file.
> > 
> > We should probably consider a re-design as we move forward in Istanbul.
> 
> It makes sense to me too to make it easier on developers of participants.

Yes, Current participant handling groups all Participant-Intermediary, Participant-Simulator and respective Participant client code for Policy-Framework, DCAE, K8S etc at a single place. This need to be distributed in upcoming release, where participant-intermediary (that wraps up dmaap communications) and respective participant code to invoke APIs into a participant-client library, and kept integrated with the component.

> Yes, Current participant handling groups all Participant-Intermediary, Participant-Simulator and respective Participant client code for Policy-Framework, DCAE, K8S etc at a single place. This need to be distributed in upcoming release, where participant-intermediary (that wraps up dmaap communications) and respective participant code to invoke APIs into a participant-client library, and kept integrated with the component.

"I thought we will have a common "participant-client" library (similar to sdc distribution client) which will wrap all the communications to other tosca-controlloop components over dmaap, rest api etc."

We do have it already, it's the participant intermediary. It is a single jar that handles the the DMaaP communication back to the tosca-controlloop server.

This change brings in the participant implementation for handling control loop related policies towards policy-api.
